data.title: Russia plans to introduce cryptocurrency regulations in July, with the aim of taking effect in 2027.
data.description: Russia plans to introduce cryptocurrency regulations in July this year and submit them to a vote by State Duma deputies. Anatoly Aksakov, Chairman of the State Duma's Financial Market Committee, stated that a comprehensive cryptocurrency legislative framework is expected to be ready by the end of June, and if passed, the relevant regulations will officially take effect on July 1, 2027. The report states that the legislation aims to allow retail and institutional investors to purchase Bitcoin by mid-2027 and establish clear regulatory requirements for cryptocurrency exchanges, with unregistered exchange operators potentially facing fines or imprisonment. According to the plan, retail investors will need to pass a qualification test, and their annual purchase limit may be capped at $4,000. The Central Bank of Russia is expected to develop a whitelist of crypto assets available for retail investors to trade, potentially covering mainstream assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum, while qualified investors will be able to trade tokens not on the list. (DL News)

data.title: The crypto market generally saw a correction, with only AI, RWA, and CeFi sectors remaining relatively resilient.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the overall crypto market is generally trending downwards. Bitcoin (BTC) fell 0.80%, dropping below $89,000; Ethereum (ETH) fell 0.62%, dropping below $3,000. Only the AI, RWA, and CeFi sectors remained relatively resilient, rising 1.81%, 0.98%, and 0.33%, respectively. Within the AI ​​sector, Worldcoin (WLD) rose 18.91%, and Kite (KITE) rose 16.09%. Within the RWA sector, Pendle (PENDLE) and Sky (SKY) rose 3.51% and 2.62%, respectively. In the CeFi sector, Binance Coin (BNB) rose 0.20%. In other sectors, the Layer 2 sector fell 0.27% in the last 24 hours, but Mantle (MNT) rose 2.41%; the Layer 1 sector fell 0.42%, with Canton Network (CC) bucking the trend and rising 6.39%; the PayFi sector fell 0.45%, with Trust Wallet (TWT) rising 1.82%; the DeFi sector fell 0.62%, but Jito (JTO) surged 20.44%; the Meme sector fell 1.46%, with PIPPIN (PIPPIN), which surged yesterday, experiencing a pullback, falling 19.66%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiDeFi, ssiRWA, and ssiAI indices rose 1.80%, 1.74%, and 1.63%, respectively.

data.title: Ethereum-Based USDT Activity Surges Amid Bitcoin Price Decline
data.description: Active addresses for Ethereum-based USDT have reached a new 30-day moving average high of 300,000, indicating heightened on-chain activity. According to NS3.AI, this increase in activity aligns with Bitcoin's inability to break the $92,000 resistance level, leading to a drop in its price. The rise in Ethereum-based USDT activity is attributed to the movement of funds into DeFi protocols and self-custody wallets on the Ethereum network, rather than centralized exchanges.

data.title: Tesla to Invest $2 Billion in Elon Musk's AI Company xAI
data.description:
Tesla announced on Wednesday its plan to invest $2 billion in xAI, an artificial intelligence company led by CEO Elon Musk. According to PANews, the company also confirmed that the production of its Cybercab robotaxi will proceed as scheduled this year. This move aligns with Musk's strategy to transition Tesla from an electric vehicle manufacturer to an AI-focused company, a shift crucial to its valuation of approximately $1.5 trillion.

Tesla's Chief Financial Officer Vaibhav Taneja revealed that the company is also accelerating the production of humanoid robots as part of a series of factory investments. The capital expenditure for this year is expected to exceed $20 billion, more than double the $8.5 billion projected for 2025.

data.title: Russia to Implement Cryptocurrency Regulations by July 2027
data.description:
Russia is set to introduce cryptocurrency regulations by July 2027, allowing both retail and institutional investors to purchase Bitcoin. According to PANews, the legislative framework is expected to be ready by the end of June for a vote by the State Duma. If approved, the law will take effect on July 1, 2027.

Anatoly Aksakov, Chairman of the State Duma's Financial Markets Committee, stated that the legislation will include regulations for cryptocurrency exchanges. Unregistered operators may face fines or imprisonment, similar to Russia's laws against illegal banking activities. Retail investors will need to pass a qualification test to trade cryptocurrencies and may face an annual purchase limit of $4,000.

The central bank plans to create a whitelist of cryptocurrencies available for retail trading, potentially including major assets like Bitcoin and Ethereum. Qualified investors will have the option to trade tokens not on the whitelist.

data.title: FOMC Meetings Influence Bitcoin's Short-Term Volatility
data.description: An analyst from XWIN Research Japan has observed that FOMC meetings serve as catalysts for clearing excessive market positions rather than setting Bitcoin's medium-term trend. According to NS3.AI, historical data indicates that Bitcoin does not exhibit consistent directional movement following these meetings. Instead, sharp short-term fluctuations are primarily driven by the liquidation of over-leveraged positions. The key factors influencing Bitcoin's near-term trajectory include the reduction of leverage, easing of selling pressure, and recovery of liquidity, rather than political developments or rate-setting outcomes.

data.title: COMEX Silver Inventories Decline Amid Rising Demand
data.description: Silver inventories in COMEX warehouses have decreased by 34 million ounces, reaching 415 million ounces, the lowest level since March 2025. The Kobeissi Letter posted on X. The stockpile has seen a significant drop of 117 million ounces, or 22%, since its peak in September. This decline in silver inventories suggests a strong physical demand as short sellers face challenges in sourcing actual metal to fulfill their futures contracts. When traders are unable to secure sufficient physical silver, they are compelled to pay higher prices set by sellers. This situation drives silver prices up, prompting more traders to purchase to avoid losses, thereby tightening market conditions further. The silver short-squeeze is currently in full effect.

data.title: Startale Group Secures $13 Million Investment from Sony Innovation Fund
data.description: Startale Group has announced a $13 million additional investment from the Sony Innovation Fund (SIF). According to Foresight News, this funding will be directed towards developing an on-chain ecosystem focused on IP-driven entertainment and AI sectors. Previously, the two parties established a joint venture, Sony Block Solutions Labs, and have been collaboratively developing the Ethereum Layer 2 network, Soneium.

data.title: Sony Innovation Fund invests an additional $13 million in blockchain solutions provider Startale Group.
data.description: Startale Group announced the completion of its Series A funding round, raising $13 million, with follow-on investment from Sony Innovation Fund. The company's primary business is building on-chain infrastructure based on the Soneium ecosystem, with products including the Startale App (integrated wallet and asset management) and the stablecoin Startale USD (USDSC). Startale Group currently operates the Ethereum Layer 2 network Soneium in a joint venture with Sony Group and also operates Astar Network. This funding round will be used to advance the development of on-chain infrastructure that integrates AI with entertainment and intellectual property.

data.title: GOAT Network Launches Bitcoin zkRollup Testnet V3
data.description:
GOAT Network has announced the official launch of its GOAT BitVM2 Testnet V3. According to Foresight News, this testnet utilizes BitVM2 technology as the final adjudication layer, aiming to enable Bitcoin's participation in financial activities without relying on custodians, committees, or multi-signature trust assumptions. The core architecture employs zero-knowledge validity proofs to ensure execution correctness, anchoring sorting results on the Bitcoin mainnet, with withdrawals and disputes ultimately adjudicated by Bitcoin mainnet rules.

Testnet V3 features key optimizations such as off-chain execution, Bitcoin-anchored sorting, and a Bitcoin-adjudicated withdrawal and dispute mechanism. It will later introduce BitVM2-GC to significantly reduce challenge costs, making security assumptions verifiable in practice for the first time. Additionally, GOAT Network has developed a comprehensive technology stack that includes Type-1 zkEVM, a decentralized sequencer, and its proprietary zkVM proof engine, Ziren.

data.title: Tokenized Gold and Silver Market Surpasses $6 Billion
data.description: The market capitalization of tokenized gold and silver has exceeded $6 billion, according to Coingecko data. According to PANews, the current market value stands at approximately $6.295 billion, marking a new historical high. Tokenized gold accounts for about $5.814 billion of the total market capitalization, while tokenized silver is valued at approximately $481 million.

data.title: Rick Rieder Emerges as Potential Federal Reserve Candidate Amid Political Donation History
data.description:
Rick Rieder, a senior executive at BlackRock, is considered a leading candidate to head the Federal Reserve. According to Odaily, his past financial support for U.S. President Donald Trump's main political opponents and prominent Democrats could influence Trump's decision to appoint him. Data from Polymarket indicates a 32% probability of Rieder being nominated by Trump.

Federal Election Commission (FEC) records reveal Rieder's bipartisan donation history. In 2020, he supported Democrats Pete Buttigieg and Cory Booker, and in 2016, he backed Jeb Bush. Notably, Rieder has not financially supported Trump in the 2016, 2020, or 2024 elections.

Rieder's donation recipients include Democratic leaders such as Chuck Schumer, Hakeem Jeffries, and Mark Warner, as well as Republican figures like Mitt Romney and Paul Ryan. His political contributions began in 2005, with the largest donation since the 2020 election being $15,825 to a group supporting Nikki Haley in December 2023.

Neither Rick Rieder nor BlackRock representatives have commented on the matter.

data.title: A whale is suspected of selling approximately 18,800 ETH in the past week, incurring a cumulative loss of approximately $3.14 million.
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ai (@ai_9684xtpa), a whale address betting on ETH has been consistently selling recently, seemingly dumping 18,823.9 ETH in the past week, worth approximately $55.59 million, resulting in a current paper loss of approximately $3.138 million. Data shows that this address deposited 5,133.91 ETH into OKX in the past 11 hours, worth approximately $15.38 million. This address last concentratedly accumulated ETH positions on January 22nd, and since December 5th, 2025, it has accumulated a total of 55,262.16 ETH, worth approximately $172 million, with an average cost of approximately $3,120 per ETH.

data.title: Rick Rieder, a leading candidate for Federal Reserve Chair, has long funded Trump's political opponents.
data.description: Rick Rieder, a BlackRock executive, is currently a leading candidate to head the Federal Reserve, but his past funding of Trump's main political opponents and top Democrats could be a potential variable in Trump's decision to appoint him, given his emphasis on loyalty. According to Polymarket data, Rieder has a 32% chance of being nominated by Trump. Federal Election Commission (FEC) data shows that Rieder has a history of cross-party donations, having funded Democrats Pete Buttigieg and Cory Booker in 2020 and Jeb Bush in 2016. Importantly, he has never provided financial support to Trump in the 2016, 2020, and 2024 elections. FEC records also show that Rieder's donations have included top Democrats such as Chuck Schumer, Hakeem Jeffries, and Mark Warner, as well as Republican establishment figures like Mitt Romney and Paul Ryan. Her political donation history began in 2005, and her largest donation since the 2020 election was $15,825 to a group supporting Nikki Haley in December 2023. Representatives for Rick Rieder and BlackRock declined to comment. (Jinshi)

data.title: Coinbase joins the Trump Accounts program, planning to provide $1,000 in Bitcoin to employees' children.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that the company has joined the government's Trump Accounts program, matching eligible employees' children with a $1,000 subsidy provided by the U.S. Treasury Department. Armstrong said Coinbase hopes to distribute the subsidy in Bitcoin. The Trump Accounts program aims to establish tax-advantaged investment accounts for U.S. citizens under the age of 18 to support long-term financial planning. Currently, many large companies, including Uber, Nvidia, Intel, Broadcom, IBM, and Schwab, have participated in the program and incorporated it into their employee benefits.

data.title: The total market capitalization of tokenized gold and silver has surpassed $6 billion, setting a new all-time high.
data.description: According to Coingecko data, the total market capitalization of tokenized gold and silver has surpassed the $6 billion mark, currently standing at approximately $6.295 billion, continuing to set new historical highs. Tokenized gold accounts for approximately $5.814 billion, representing the majority of the market, while tokenized silver accounts for approximately $481 million, with both sectors showing upward trends.

data.title: Scallop's Progress and Future Plans Highlighted by CEO Kris Lai
data.description: Kris Lai, founder and CEO of Scallop, recently shared insights into the project's progress, unique features, and future plans. According to NS3.AI, Scallop is positioned as a Sui-based decentralized finance (DeFi) platform. The discussion focused on raising awareness about Scallop's innovations and upcoming developments.

data.title: Lighter Initiates Process for Accessing LLP via LIT Staking
data.description:
Lighter has announced on the X platform that it has begun the process of accessing LLP through LIT staking, with approximately 56 million LIT already staked. According to Odaily, under the new rules, each staked LIT can cover 10 USDC of LLP quota, and any LLP configuration exceeding this coverage will gradually be returned to users. Starting tomorrow, up to 3% of the uncovered amount, with a maximum of 100 USDC, will be returned daily to the account's USDC balance.

Lighter stated that this move aims to align the interests of LIT stakers and LLP holders. Additionally, the LLP allocation rules are now verifiable and have been integrated into the ZK circuit. The platform also announced that in two weeks, traders will be allowed to use LLP as collateral to enhance overall capital efficiency and expand the use cases for LLP and staked LIT.

data.title: The US crypto industry raised approximately $193 million for the midterm elections.
data.description: The US crypto industry has raised approximately $193 million for the upcoming midterm elections, setting a new record for the sector. The Fairshake Political Action Committee (PAC) disclosed that this round of fundraising included $25 million each from Coinbase and Ripple, and approximately $24 million from a16z, placing it among the most financially powerful political forces in the US. The report states that in the last election cycle, Fairshake and its affiliates spent over $130 million supporting pro-crypto candidates. In addition, the Winklevoss brothers, co-founders of Gemini, donated approximately $21 million worth of Bitcoin to the Digital Freedom Fund PAC, and another major PAC, with an executive from Cantor Fitzgerald serving as its treasurer, also stated that it has raised approximately $100 million to support pro-crypto candidates. (Financial Times)

data.title: Tesla did not adjust its Bitcoin holdings in the fourth quarter, recognizing approximately $239 million in digital asset impairment losses.
data.description: Tesla did not adjust its Bitcoin holdings in the fourth quarter of 2025, maintaining its position at 11,509 Bitcoins. However, due to the price of Bitcoin falling from approximately $114,000 to $88,000 during the quarter, Tesla confirmed an after-tax impairment loss of approximately $239 million on its digital assets in its latest earnings report. The report recalls that Tesla disclosed holding approximately 43,200 Bitcoins in February 2021, subsequently selling approximately 75% of its holdings in 2022, after which its Bitcoin balance remained relatively stable. The earnings report also showed that Tesla's fourth-quarter revenue was $24.9 billion, lower than the market expectation of $25.1 billion, but adjusted earnings per share were $0.50, higher than the consensus expectation of $0.45, and the stock price rose approximately 3.4% in after-hours trading. (CoinDesk)
